questions.question: Explain the hydrological cycle with a neat labeled diagram. Also mention the role of human activities in altering this natural cycle.
keyPoints: Evaporation from water bodies
Transpiration from plants
Condensation to form clouds
Precipitation (rain/snow)
Runoff and infiltration
Human impacts: deforestation, urbanization, dam construction

questions.question: Define and distinguish between: (i) Surface water and groundwater (ii) Confined and unconfined aquifer
keyPoints: Surface water: visible on surface (rivers, lakes)
Groundwater: subsurface water in aquifers
Unconfined aquifer: water table at top, direct recharge
Confined aquifer: sandwiched between impermeable layers, under pressure

questions.question: Describe the water quality parameters: pH, DO, BOD, and TDS. Give their permissible limits for drinking water.
keyPoints: pH: 6.5-8.5 (acid-alkaline balance)
DO: >5 mg/L (for aquatic life)
BOD: <5 mg/L (low organic pollution)
TDS: <500 mg/L (WHO guideline) or <1000 mg/L (acceptable)

questions.question: What is watershed management? Discuss its objectives and major components.
keyPoints: Watershed: drainage basin area
Objectives: soil conservation, water conservation, sustainable development
Components: land use planning, water resource development, erosion control, vegetation management

questions.question: Discuss the causes and consequences of water pollution. Suggest two measures for water conservation.
keyPoints: Causes: industrial effluent, agricultural runoff, sewage disposal
Consequences: health hazards, aquatic ecosystem damage, bioaccumulation
Conservation: rainwater harvesting, watershed management, wastewater treatment

questions.question: Explain the methods of water resource development. Differentiate between conventional and non-conventional methods with suitable examples.
keyPoints: Conventional: dams, reservoirs, canals, tube wells
Non-conventional: rainwater harvesting, desalination, wastewater reuse
Advantages and limitations of each
Need for sustainable water management

questions.question: Describe the procedure for artificial groundwater recharge. Discuss its significance in context of over-extraction of groundwater.
keyPoints: Methods: injection wells, spreading basins, recharge trenches
Need: declining water tables, seawater intrusion
Benefits: increased storage, water quality improvement
Challenges: land availability, water quality, cost

questions.question: A river water sample has the following parameters: pH = 5.5, DO = 2 mg/L, BOD = 180 mg/L, TDS = 1200 mg/L. Analyze this data and comment on the water quality. Is it suitable for drinking and aquatic life?
keyPoints: pH 5.5: acidic (below acceptable range 6.5-8.5)
DO 2 mg/L: very low (stresses aquatic life, below 5 mg/L)
BOD 180 mg/L: highly polluted (>200 threshold)
TDS 1200 mg/L: exceeds WHO limit of 500 mg/L
Conclusion: heavily polluted, not suitable for drinking or aquatic life
